string.name: app_name
string.description: Application name. [CHAR_LIMIT=30]

string.name: title_activity_vr
string.description: VR activity title. [CHAR_LIMIT=30]

string.name: app_name_dev
string.description: Short (max 20 charaters) text for the application name for dev builds. [CHAR_LIMIT=50]

string.name: app_name_prod
string.description: Short (max 20 charaters) text for the application name. [CHAR_LIMIT=30]

string.name: app_name_dogfood
string.description: Short (max 20 charaters) text for the application name for dogfood builds. [CHAR_LIMIT=30]

string.name: switch_viewer
string.description: Short text shown on the settings menu to let the user switch viewer. [CHAR_LIMIT=30]

string.name: welcome_pair
string.description: Welcome message prompting the user that they will need to pair their viewer [CHAR LIMIT=80]

resources.string: Pair your phone with your viewer for the best experience.

string.name: setup_device
string.description: Short text (max 50 chars) displayed as a label to invite the user to start pairing their Cardboard viewer with their phone.

string.name: yes
string.description: Short text (less than 10 chars) confirming that the current viewer is correct.

string.name: get_cardboard
string.description: Short text (less than 15 chars) displayed on a button next to the no_viewer text. A call to action to get a Cardboard viewer.

string.name: no_cardboard
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) asking if the user has a cardboard viewer.

string.name: app_grid_view_name
string.description: Displayed on the tab of My Library page

string.name: bundled_demos_label
string.description: Short text (max 30 chars) shown on button to start the demos that are bundled with the Cardboard application, such as Windy Day, Tour Guide, etc

string.name: error_opening_app
string.description: Error message shown when unable to launch another app from Cardboard

string.name: help
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) for menu item in the Settings menu, to get help about the app.

string.name: about
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) for menu item in the Settings menu, to know more about the app.

string.name: privacy_policy
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) for menu item in the Settings menu, to know more about the app.

string.name: contact_us
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) for menu item in the Settings menu, to contact the app. [CHAR_LIMIT=50]

string.name: view_web_page_failed
string.description: Short text (less than 30 chars) for menu item in the Settings menu, to know more about the app. [CHAR_LIMIT=50]

resources.string: Could not open the requested web page.

string.name: switch_viewer_action
string.description: Text which when pressed, takes the user to the view that allows them to switch their Cardboard compatible viewer. [CHAR LIMIT=10]

string.name: switch_viewer_prompt
string.description: Prompt asking if the user wants to switch their Cardboard compatible viewer. [CHAR LIMIT=45]

string.name: place_your_phone_into_cardboard
string.description: Message asking the user to place their phone into Cardboard. This is a generic message used in the absence of knowing the current viewer. [CHAR LIMIT=50]

resources.string: Place your phone into your Cardboard viewer

string.name: register_headset_button
string.description: Short text (max 30 chars) shown on menu and button to let the user pair their phone to another Cardboard compatible viewer. Once the viewer is paired, all applications that use our SDK will be optimized for this viewer.

string.name: headset_presentation
string.description: Text (max 60 chars) shown in the settings page that explains which Cardboard-compatible viewer is currently paired with the user's phone.

resources.string: Your phone is configured for the following viewer:

string.name: default_headset_model
string.description: Short text (max 30 chars) that is shown when the Cardboard viewer currently paired is the default one.

string.name: headset_vendor_prefix
string.description: Short text displayed when describing the Cardboard viewer currently paired. It is placed before displaying the name of the viewer manufacturer, as in 'Cardboard v1 *from* Google LLC' or 'ViewerMaster *from* Mattel, Inc.'. [CHAR_LIMIT=10]

string.name: unknown_headset_info
string.description: Short text for label when the Cardboard viewer currently paired with the phone is unknown. [CHAR_LIMIT=14]

string.name: read_media_permission_rationale
string.description: Text displayed in the permission rationale dialog. It explains that we need access to storage in order to load images and videos for the Photosphere and MyVideos demo experiences, respectively. [CHAR LIMIT=NONE]

resources.string: Storage permission is required to load images and videos for the Photosphere and MyVideos demo experiences, respectively.

string.name: no_video_notice
string.description: Text (max 100 characters) in 'My Videos' demo shown when the user doesn't have any videos on their phone. [CHAR LIMIT=140]

resources.string: Take videos with your phone and watch them here

string.name: playing_video
string.description: Small Text (max 20 characters) in 'My Videos' demo shown on top of a video thumbnail to indicate that this particular video is being played on the main screen. [CHAR LIMIT=30]

string.name: rate_the_app
string.description: Short text for menu item in the About page, to rate the Cardboard application on its Play Store page. [CHAR_LIMIT=24]

string.name: terms_of_service
string.description: Short text for menu item in the About page, to see the terms of service. [CHAR_LIMIT=32]

string.name: product_safety
string.description: Short text for menu item in the About page, to link out to the product safety information page (google.com/get/cardboard/product-safety/). [CHAR_LIMIT=28]

string.name: licenses
string.description: Short text for menu item in the About page, to see the open source licenses and copyrights. [CHAR_LIMIT=16]

string.name: app_version
string.description: Short text for menu item in the About page, to specify the app's current version. [CHAR_LIMIT=22]
